channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: EXCLUSIVE VIDEO: AOC refuses to say whether Democrats' rhetoric is responsible for Tesla attacks
item.description: Democrat Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez refused to answer whether Democrats' inflammatory rhetoric against Elon Musk is linked to attacks against Tesla owners and dealers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariffs: Here's how much countries are getting hit
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday announced he was imposing reciprocal tariffs on dozens of nations, citing what he called years of unfair trade practices.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Judge dismisses corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge dismissed New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case on Wednesday, agreeing it was the only practical outcome while blasting the Justice Department’s “troubling” rationale for wanting the charges thrown out — namely so the Democrat could help President Donald Tru
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats demoralized by Trump get a boost from Wisconsin voters and Cory Booker's speech
item.description: Democratic leaders are sharing a rare moment of relief — if not celebration — as they cling to election results from Wisconsin and Florida that offered a glimmer of hope for their party.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal judge questions whether EPA move to rapidly cancel 'green bank' grants was legal
item.description: U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan on Wednesday questioned a Department of Justice attorney over whether the Environmental Protection Agency violated the law when it terminated $20 billion in green bank grants allegedly without following the proper process.
item.dc:creator: Michael Phillis, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Not so fast-Musk will finish the job
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump touts return of the 'American Dream' in historic tariff announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump announced his highly anticipated trade plan from the Rose Garden of the White House on Wednesday, which he has declared "Liberation Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security's acting leader faces calls to resign over decision to cut Maine contracts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration's acting commissioner is facing calls to resign after he issued an order — which was quickly rescinded — that would have required Maine parents to register their newborns for Social Security numbers at a federal office rather than the hospital.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Naval Academy tosses 400 books from library following Trump DEI expulsion orders
item.description: The U.S. Naval Academy is pitching almost 400 books from its library, in accordance with directives from the Trump administration to eliminate content related to diversity, equity and inclusion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court blocks DOGE effort to access Social Security data
item.description: A federal judge on March 20 temporarily blocked billionaire Elon Musk’s DOGE from Social Security systems.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Dems foreshadow more forced votes to block Trump's emergency orders
item.description: Senate Democrats may move to challenge additional Trump emergencies and force floor votes on them, Sen. Tim Kaine suggested at a press conference on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SCOOP: Lindsey Graham amendment signals GOP budget breakthrough, sets stage for Trump agenda
item.description: Budget Chair Lindsey Graham released the Senate's amendment to the House-passed reconciliation resolution, signaling movement on Trump's agenda in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Army identifies 4th soldier who died in training accident in Lithuania
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Army has released the identity of the fourth soldier who died in a training accident in Lithuania, a day after his body was recovered during a weeklong search that took hundreds of troops and other rescue workers from three nations.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No bathroom breaks: How Booker accomplished record Senate speech
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) set a new record for the longest Senate speech in history on Monday and Tuesday by holding the upper chamber’s floor for more than 25 hours.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin and Florida elections provide early warning signs to Trump and Republicans
item.description: A trio of spring elections offered early warning signs to Republicans and President Donald Trump, as Democrats rallied against his efforts to slash the federal government and the outsize role being played by billionaire Elon Musk in the early days of his new administration.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MAYA SWEEDLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: 'Shady trial lawyer pipeline' funneling millions to Democrats, according to report
item.description: One of the country’s leading consumer protection firms released a report exposing a “shady lawyer pipeline" of politicians handing out lucrative public contracts to trial lawyers who contribute millions to liberal campaigns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 4 key takeaways as Trump’s sweeping HHS layoffs begin
item.description: In total, the layoffs, part of the agency restructuring plan publicly announced late last week, are anticipated to impact as many as 10,000 staffers.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Auditor reports that Minnesota muni liquor store profits up, in part due to THC offerings
item.description: Minnesota State Auditor Julie Blaha issued her annual report on municipal-run liquor store sales. It was the first include sales of hemp-derived THC beverages and gummies, and operators suggested those items contributed to store profits in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Outrage grows over Maryland man's mistaken deportation to El Salvador prison
item.description: In the 22 days since Maryland resident Kilmar Abrego Garcia was mistakenly deported to a notoriously violent prison in El Salvador, his young autistic son has sought comfort in the scent of his missing father’s clothes.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal judge drops corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: Adams was scheduled to go on trial in April until new leadership at the Justice Department under the Trump administration ordered prosecutors in New York in February to drop the case, sparking a public outcry and resignations of prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas and Carrie Johnson, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariff tumult has ripples for sporting goods, puts costly hockey gear in price-hike crosshair
item.description: The sports equipment industry is no exception to the headaches being caused throughout the U.S. commercial ecosystem by the promise of 25 percent tariffs on Canadian imports by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 6 things borrowers should know about federal student loans right now
item.description: NPR has spent the past few weeks catching up with student loan experts and asking the Trump administration for clarity on some of borrowers’ biggest questions.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘very angry’ and ‘pissed off’ at Putin during NBC News interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was “very angry” and “pissed off” when Russian President Vladimir Putin criticized the credibility of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s leadership, adding that the comments were “not going in the right location.
item.dc:creator: Kristin Welker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Congress questions the FAA, US Army and NTSB over deadly midair collision in DC
item.description: The head of the National Transportation Safety Board appeared alongside officials from the Federal Aviation Administration and the Army's aviation division at a hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5
